---
title: "Society of Insect Welfare - Literature Dashboard"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: scroll
    theme: minty  
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load all necessary packages
library(flexdashboard)
library(tidyverse)
library(visNetwork)
library(igraph)
library(tidytext)
library(ggwordcloud)
library(viridis)
library(plotly) # <--- NUEVA LIBRERÍA PARA EL MAPA

# Load data
datos2 <- read_csv("Gallery_Library.csv") %>%
  mutate(ID_Articulo = row_number())

# Cargar datos de miembros (ajusta el nombre del archivo si es diferente)
Members <- read_csv("Members.csv")

# Load data
datos <- read_csv("Gallery_Library.csv") %>%
  mutate(ID_Articulo = row_number())
```


Row {data-height=500}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Top 15 Most frequent topics {data-width=600}

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 2: TOP 15 MOST FREQUENT TOPICS
# ==============================================================================
# Since the "Topics" column contains several topics separated by commas, 
# we need to separate them to count them individually.

grafico_temas <- datos %>%
  # Remove rows without topics
  filter(!is.na(Topics)) %>% 
  # Separate the Topics column into multiple rows (using the comma as separator)
  separate_rows(Topics, sep = ",") %>% 
  # Remove extra whitespaces at the beginning and end
  mutate(Topics = str_trim(Topics)) %>% 
  # Count the frequency of each topic and sort from highest to lowest
  count(Topics, sort = TRUE) %>% 
  # Take only the top 15 topics
  slice_max(order_by = n, n = 15) %>% 
  
  # Create the plot
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Topics, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#55A868", alpha = 0.8) +
  coord_flip() + # Flip the bars so the texts are easier to read
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 15 Most Frequent Topics in the Literature",
    x = "Category / Topic",
    y = "Number of Articles"
  )

# Show the plot
print(grafico_temas)

```


### Word Cloud of Article Titles Row {data-height=300}

```{r}
data("stop_words")

datos %>%
  filter(!is.na(Title)) %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, Title) %>%
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") %>%
  filter(!word %in% c("evaluation", "effects", "effect", "study", "studies"),
         !str_detect(word, "^[0-9]+$")) %>% 
  mutate(word = case_when(
    word %in% c("animal", "animals") ~ "animals",
    word %in% c("insect", "insects") ~ "insects",
    word %in% c("invertebrate", "invertebrates") ~ "invertebrates",
    word %in% c("anesthesia", "anesthetics", "anesthetic", 
                "anaesthesia", "anaesthetics", "anaesthetic") ~ "anesthesia",
    TRUE ~ word 
  )) %>%
  count(word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = n, n = 50) %>%
  ggplot(aes(label = word, size = n, color = n)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud_area(shape = "circle") +
  scale_size_area(max_size =20) + 
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#4C72B0", high = "#C44E52") + 
  theme_minimal()
```


Row {data-height=500}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### Evolution of publicacions per year {data-width=300}


```{r}

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 1: EVOLUTION OF PUBLICATIONS PER YEAR
# ==============================================================================
# This chart will help us see if interest in this field has grown over time.

grafico_anios <- datos %>%
  # Remove rows with no publication year
  filter(!is.na(`Publication Year`)) %>% 
  # Count how many publications there are for each year
  count(`Publication Year`) %>% 
  
  # Create the plot
  ggplot(aes(x = `Publication Year`, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4C72B0", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) + # Add the number on top of the bar
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Evolution of Publications per Year",
    subtitle = "Literature trend on invertebrates, pain, and welfare",
    x = "Publication Year",
    y = "Number of Publications"
  )

# Show the plot
print(grafico_anios)
```


### Evolution of the Field (Article Types) {data-width=300}

```{r}
datos %>%
  filter(!is.na(Topics), !is.na(`Publication Year`)) %>%
  select(`Publication Year`, Topics) %>%
  separate_rows(Topics, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(Topics = str_trim(Topics)) %>%
  filter(str_detect(Topics, "Article Type")) %>%
  mutate(Topics = str_remove(Topics, "Article Type - ")) %>%
  mutate(Topics = fct_lump(Topics, n = 5)) %>% 
  filter(Topics != "Other") %>%
  count(`Publication Year`, Topics) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Publication Year`, y = n, fill = Topics)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "plasma") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Articles", fill = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```


Row {data-height=500}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Evolution of the Top 5 Topics over time {data-width=300}
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 4: EVOLUTION OF THE TOP 5 TOPICS OVER TIME
# ==============================================================================

# First, we identify the Top 5 topics (excluding "Article Type" categories)
top_temas <- datos %>%
  filter(!is.na(Topics)) %>%
  separate_rows(Topics, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(Topics = str_trim(Topics)) %>%
  # Exclude tags that are just article types to focus on real subjects
  filter(!str_detect(Topics, "Article Type")) %>%
  count(Topics, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = n, n = 6) %>%
  filter (Topics != "Invertebrate")%>% # Estou asumindo que todos sao de invertebrados..
  pull(Topics)

# Now we plot how these 5 topics evolved over the years
grafico_evolucion_temas <- datos %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Publication Year`), !is.na(Topics)) %>%
  separate_rows(Topics, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(Topics = str_trim(Topics)) %>%
  filter(Topics %in% top_temas) %>% # Filter only the top 5
  count(`Publication Year`, Topics) %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = `Publication Year`, y = n, color = Topics, group = Topics)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") + # Use a distinct color palette
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Evolution of Top 5 Research Topics Over Time",
    subtitle = "Tracking the growth of specific subjects (e.g., Welfare, Sentience, Farming)",
    x = "Publication Year",
    y = "Number of Publications",
    color = "Topic"
  )

# Show the plot
print(grafico_evolucion_temas)
```

### Most prolific authors {data-width=300}


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 3 (Optional): MOST PROLIFIC AUTHORS
# ==============================================================================
# Authors are separated by semicolons (;)

grafico_autores <- datos %>%
  filter(!is.na(Authors)) %>%
  separate_rows(Authors, sep = ";") %>%
  mutate(Authors = str_trim(Authors)) %>%
  count(Authors, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = n, n = 15) %>% # Top 10 authors
  
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Authors, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#C44E52", alpha = 0.8) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 15 Most Prolific Authors",
    x = "Author",
    y = "Number of Articles"
  )

# Show the plot
print(grafico_autores)

```


Row {data-height=500}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### Interactive Researcher Co-authorship Network  {data-width=500}

```{r}
# 1. Clean and separate the authors
autores_df <- datos %>%
  select(ID_Articulo, Authors) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Authors)) %>%
  separate_rows(Authors, sep = ";") %>% 
  mutate(Authors = str_trim(Authors))   

# Calculate total articles per author for the hover tooltip
conteo_total_autores <- autores_df %>% count(Authors)

# 2. Filter authors (As requested, keeping all authors by commenting out the filter)
autores_frecuentes <- conteo_total_autores %>%
  # filter(n >= 2) %>%
  pull(Authors)

# 3. Create EDGES
edges_autores <- autores_df %>%
  filter(Authors %in% autores_frecuentes) %>%
  inner_join(autores_df %>% filter(Authors %in% autores_frecuentes), 
             by = "ID_Articulo", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  filter(Authors.x < Authors.y) %>% 
  count(Authors.x, Authors.y, name = "value") %>%
  rename(from = Authors.x, to = Authors.y) %>%
  mutate(title = paste0("<p><b>Collaborations:</b> ", value, "</p>"))

# 4. Create NODES
nombres_unicos_autores <- unique(c(edges_autores$from, edges_autores$to))

nodes_autores <- tibble(id = nombres_unicos_autores, label = nombres_unicos_autores) %>%
  left_join(conteo_total_autores, by = c("id" = "Authors")) %>%
  mutate(
    title = paste0("<p><b>Author:</b> ", id, "<br><b>Total articles:</b> ", n, "</p>"),
    color = ifelse(id %in% c("Fischer, Bob", "Barrett, Meghan"), "#FFD700", "tomato"),
    size = ifelse(id %in% c("Fischer, Bob", "Barrett, Meghan"), 35, 20),
    font.size = ifelse(id %in% c("Fischer, Bob", "Barrett, Meghan"), 20, 14)
  )

# 5. Plot the Network
visNetwork(nodes_autores, edges_autores) %>%
  visIgraphLayout(layout = "layout_with_fr") %>%
  visEdges(color = list(color = "darkgray", highlight = "black"), smooth = FALSE) %>%
  visNodes(font = list(color = "black", strokeWidth = 2, strokeColor = "white"),
           shadow = list(enabled = TRUE, size = 10)  # <--- ESTO LE DA EL EFECTO 3D
                       ) %>%
  visOptions(
    highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE), 
    nodesIdSelection = TRUE 
  ) %>%
  visInteraction(dragNodes = TRUE, dragView = TRUE, zoomView = TRUE, hover = TRUE)
```

### Topic Co-occurrence Network (Interactive) {data-width=500}

```{r}
# 1. Prepare Data for the Network
temas_df <- datos2 %>%
  select(ID_Articulo, Topics) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Topics)) %>%
  separate_rows(Topics, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(Topics = str_trim(Topics)) %>%
  filter(!str_detect(Topics, "Article Type"))

temas_frecuentes <- temas_df %>% count(Topics) %>% filter(n >= 3) %>% pull(Topics)

edges_temas <- temas_df %>%
  filter(Topics %in% temas_frecuentes) %>%
  inner_join(temas_df %>% filter(Topics %in% temas_frecuentes), 
             by = "ID_Articulo", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  filter(Topics.x < Topics.y) %>%
  count(Topics.x, Topics.y, name = "value") %>%
  rename(from = Topics.x, to = Topics.y) %>%
  mutate(title = paste0("<p><b>Appeared together:</b> ", value, " times</p>"))

conteo_temas <- temas_df %>% count(Topics)
nombres_temas <- unique(c(edges_temas$from, edges_temas$to))

nodes_temas <- tibble(id = nombres_temas, label = nombres_temas) %>%
  left_join(conteo_temas, by = c("id" = "Topics")) %>%
  mutate(
    title = paste0("<p><b>Topic:</b> ", id, "<br><b>Total articles:</b> ", n, "</p>"),
    value = n
  )

# 2. Plot the Network
visNetwork(nodes_temas, edges_temas) %>%
  visIgraphLayout(layout = "layout_with_fr") %>%
  visEdges(color = list(color = "lightgray", highlight = "black")) %>%
  visNodes(
    color = list(background = "#88CFA6", border = "#2F784D", highlight = "#FFD700"),
    font = list(color = "black", strokeWidth = 1.5, strokeColor = "white"),
           shadow = list(enabled = TRUE, size = 10)  # <--- ESTO LE DA EL EFECTO 3D
                     
  ) %>%
  visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE),
             nodesIdSelection = TRUE) %>%
  visInteraction(dragNodes = TRUE, dragView = TRUE, zoomView = TRUE, hover = TRUE)
```


Row {data-height=600}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Global Community Map

```{r}
# 1. Preparar los datos para el mapa
datos_mapa <- Members %>%
  # Limpiar espacios en blanco por si acaso
  mutate(Country = str_trim(Country)) %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(
    Total = n(),
    # Unimos el nombre y apellido, tomando solo los primeros 15 (para no desbordar la pantalla si hay muchos)
    Lista_Nombres = paste(head(paste(`First Name`, `Last Name`), 15), collapse = "<br>")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Si hay más de 15 personas, agregamos la etiqueta "... y X más"
    Lista_Nombres = ifelse(Total > 15, 
                           paste0(Lista_Nombres, "<br><i>...and ", Total - 15, " more</i>"), 
                           Lista_Nombres),
    # Construimos el cuadro de texto flotante (Tooltip) con HTML
    Texto_Hover = paste0(
      "<b>", Country, "</b><br>",
      "<b>Total Members:</b> ", Total, "<br>",
      "-------------------------<br>",
      Lista_Nombres
    )
  )

# 2. Dibujar el mapa interactivo
plot_geo(datos_mapa) %>%
  add_trace(
    z = ~Total,                      # El color dependerá del número de miembros
    locations = ~Country,            # La columna con los nombres de los países
    locationmode = 'country names',  # Le decimos a plotly que use nombres (no coordenadas)
    text = ~Texto_Hover,             # El texto interactivo que construimos
    hoverinfo = 'text',              # Mostrar solo nuestro texto
    colorscale = 'YlGnBu',           # Escala de colores (Amarillo a Azul oscuro)
    marker = list(line = list(color = toRGB("darkgray"), width = 0.5)) # Bordes de los países
  ) %>%
  colorbar(title = "N° of Members") %>%
  layout(
    geo = list(
      showframe = FALSE,
      showcoastlines = FALSE,
      showland = TRUE,
      landcolor = toRGB("#E5E5E5"),       # Continentes en gris claro y elegante
      showocean = TRUE,
      oceancolor = toRGB("#F0F8FF"),      # Océano en un tono 'Alice Blue' súper sutil
      projection = list(type = 'natural earth') 
    ),
    # Quitamos el fondo del gráfico para que se funda con la página
    paper_bgcolor = 'rgba(0,0,0,0)',
    plot_bgcolor = 'rgba(0,0,0,0)'
  )
  )
```
